text.title: AI-Assisted Single-Interview Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ai-interview-analysis methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every highlight or code MUST cite (line_range_start, line_range_end) from the transcript.
rule.rationale: Unanchored highlights cannot be verified or retrieved later.

rule.statement: Report MUST include a contradiction section listing any conflicting statements within the interview, or 'none detected'.
rule.rationale: Contradictions are the highest-signal items for follow-up; omitting them loses research value.

rule.statement: Follow-up questions MUST be specific (cite the contradiction or gap they probe), not generic.
rule.rationale: Generic follow-ups waste the next interview slot.

rule.statement: Report MUST be reviewed by the researcher before any cross-interview synthesis.
rule.rationale: AI hallucinations propagate quickly into synthesis; this is the gate.

rule.statement: If multi-interview synthesis is the actual goal, skip and use interview-note-synthesis-ai directly.
rule.rationale: Single-interview analysis is a building block; synthesis runs different rules.
